---
title: "Fifa Data"
description: |
  Analysis of FIFA attendance data from TidyTuesday
author: Josh Goldhaber
date: September 24, 2025
format: 
  html: 
    code-fold: true
    echo: true
    warning: false
    message: false
---


```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Scatterplot comparing countries' share of world population to their share of FIFA World Cup TV audience, on a log-log scale. Each point is colored by confederation. A red dashed 45-degree line represents equal population and audience shares. Most points fall above the line, indicating that many countries have a disproportionately high TV audience relative to their population share."

library(tidyverse)

fifa <- read_csv("data/week11_fifa_audience.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

ggplot(fifa, aes(x = population_share, y = tv_audience_share, color = confederation)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 3) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  labs(
    title = "FIFA World Cup: Population Share vs. TV Audience Share (log10 scale)",
    x = "Share of World Population (log scale)",
    y = "Share of World Cup TV Audience (log scale)",
    color = "Confederation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


## Analysis:
The scatterplot reveals a strong positive relationship between a country's share of the world population and its share of the FIFA World Cup TV audience. 
Most points lie above the red dashed 45-degree line, indicating that many countries have a disproportionately high TV audience relative to their population share. 
This suggests that smaller countries, in terms of population, often punch above their weight in terms of World Cup viewership. 
Confederations also show distinct patterns, with some regions consistently outperforming others in audience share relative to population.


## Source:
FIFA. FIFA Audience Data. TidyTuesday, rfordatascience, 12 June 2018.
https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/cd0df1831dc506ea0a6f1295d7605142120d6dbd/data/2018/2018-06-12/week11_fifa_audience.csv
